channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration is set to drop lawsuit pushing Idaho to allow emergency abortions, filing shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is expected to drop a federal lawsuit pushing for emergency abortions in Idaho, which has a strict ban on the procedure, according to court documents filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of Mexico's lawsuit against American gunmakers
item.description: Mexico argues it should be allowed to sue American gunmakers for their weapons being smuggled into the country and used by cartels, but the U.S. Supreme Court appears skeptical.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem says DHS will 'not be deterred' after ICE hit by new leaks ahead of Virginia raid
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem is responding to leaks about a raid in Northern Virginia, after officials have said they are trying to crack down on leaks to the media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats seek to probe Musk conflicts and DOGE firings with resolutions of inquiry in the House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats on the House Oversight Committee are introducing a pair of resolutions demanding the Trump administration turn over documents and information about billionaire adviser Elon Musk's potential conflicts of interest and the firings of federal workers, The Associated Press has
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to watch in Trump's primetime address to Congress
item.description: Five things to watch as President Donald Trump gives his first major primetime address to Congress and the nation since returning to power in the White House six weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon nominee pushed to acknowledge Russian invasion of Ukraine in Senate hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s nominee for the top policy job at the Pentagon acknowledged during his confirmation hearing on Tuesday that Russia invaded Ukraine and poses a significant military threat to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats investigate DOJ for dropping charges on Mayor Adams
item.description: The lawmakers wrote a letter to Attorney General Pam Bondi on Sunday, accusing the DOJ of trying to cover up a quid pro quo.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department is reviewing prosecution of Colorado clerk who supported Trump’s election lies
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The Department of Justice is backing a former county election clerk in Colorado who was convicted for her role in allowing supporters of President Donald Trump to access confidential data about the 2020 election, the latest move by the administration to use its power to reward allies w
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINA CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Zelensky calls Trump meeting 'regrettable,' stops short of apology
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday called his contentious meeting last week with President Trump at the White House a "regrettable" gathering but stopped short of issuing an apology for the public blowup.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Trump administration is affecting the U.S. Refugee Admissions Program
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the U.S. Refugee Admissions Program and Minnesota’s history of refugee resettlement.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: President Trump pauses Ukraine military aid
item.description: The news comes just days after a disastrous meeting between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: A 10% tariff on Chinese imports was also doubled to 20%, and Beijing has retaliated with tariffs of up to 15% on a wide array of U.S. farm exports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why Trump's joint remarks to Congress won't be a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday night will stand at the front of the U.S. House chamber to address a joint session of Congress, the first of his second term in office.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s speech to Congress comes as he wields vast power almost daring lawmakers, courts to stop him
item.description: President Donald Trump arrives this week on Capitol Hill to deliver a speech to Congress, a coequal branch of government he has bulldozed past this first month in office, wielding unimaginable executive power to get what he wants, at home and abroad.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
